title: Genista crotalarioides
intro: Genista crotalarioides, also known as Scorpius broom or broom of the scorpion, is a plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Genista crotalarioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Genista crotalarioides is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function.
taxonomy: Genista crotalarioides is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a legume native to the Iberian Peninsula and northwestern Africa. The plant is often classified as a member of the subgenus Genista, within the genus Genista. The subgenus Genista is further divided into several species, with Genista crotalarioides being one of the distinct species. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
